tu.prop: Whole-poem fallback because Chinese and English stanza structures do not align safely.

tuv.seg:
習習谷風，維風及雨。 將恐將懼，維予與女。 將安將樂，女轉棄予。
習習谷風，維風及頹。 將恐將懼，寘予于懷。 將安將樂，棄予如遺。
習習谷風，維山崔嵬。 無草不死，無木不萎。 忘我大德，思我小怨。

el d r ou est all
2 Gently blows the east wind; — And the wind is followed by the tornado. In the time of fear and dread, You placed me in your breast. In your time of rest and pleasure, You have cast me off like an abandoned thing.
3 Gently blows the east wind； — And on the rock- covered tops of the hills, There is no grass which is not dying, No tree wliich is not withering. You forget my great virtues, And think of my small faults. VIII. Luh ngo,
1 Long and large grows the ngo ; — It is not the ngo but the haou. Alas ! alas ! my parents, With what toil ye gave me birth!
